data.body: ROOT CAUSE (corrects the ticket's assumption): show never emits empty-on-success — it returns the complete record (rc0) or an error envelope (rc1). The empty-stdout-exit-0 is the CONSUMER's view of a multi-second STALL: show's read path runs ensure_fresh→reconverge, which does git fetch then acquires the write-lock (15s) BEFORE reading; a concurrent background push holding that lock stalled show ~15s (proven: ensure_fresh measured 15.1s under a held lock). The unaffected reads (clarity-check/check-ac) skip reconverge entirely. FIX (branch worktree-civil-marlin-flare, commit 9943f1e1): read-path reconverge now waits only 2s for the lock (prefer the local snapshot) and claims the throttle marker before reconverging so a burst doesn't all pile up. Reads are also immune to a stale mkdir lock now. Mutual-exclusion semantics untouched. Pending PR/merge before close.
